main-sections.observatory-description: Discover the universe from our stationary observing station, equipped with advanced telescopes.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Personal data processing policy of the “Look at the Stars” project: what data we collect at sign-in, why, how we store it, and how to withdraw consent or unsubscribe.
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy (the “Policy”) sets out how personal data of users of the “Look at the Stars” project website (the “Site”) is processed and protected by the personal data Operator - Orenburg-Media (the online publication “Orenburg Media”, the “Operator”; registered address: office 402, 11 Pobedy Ave., Orenburg, 460021, Russia; contact address: orenburgmedia@yandex.ru) - while a user uses the Site. The Policy is prepared in accordance with Russian Federal Law No. 152-FZ of 27 July 2006 “On Personal Data”.
privacy.general-text: The Operator's processing of personal data is based on the principles of lawfulness of the purposes and methods of processing, as well as good faith and fairness. The Policy applies to all personal data the Operator receives about Site users, regardless of the form in which it is provided.
privacy.terms-intro: The following terms are used in this Policy:
privacy.term-operator-text: - Orenburg-Media (the online publication “Orenburg Media”), which independently organizes and/or carries out the processing of Site users' personal data and determines the purposes of processing, the composition of the data processed, and the actions performed with it.
privacy.term-personal-data-text: - any information relating directly or indirectly to an identified or identifiable Site user.
privacy.term-processing-text: - any action or set of actions performed with personal data: collection, recording, systematization, accumulation, storage, clarification, extraction, use, transfer, depersonalization, blocking, deletion, destruction.
privacy.term-consent-text: - a voluntary, specific, informed and unambiguous expression of will by which the user gives consent to the processing of their personal data.
privacy.term-cookie-text: - small text files stored in the user's browser that allow the Site to recognize the user on repeat visits.
privacy.operator-rights-title: 3. Rights and obligations of the Operator
privacy.operator-rights-subtitle: The Operator has the right to:
privacy.operator-right-1: obtain reliable information from the user that is necessary for processing personal data;
privacy.operator-right-2: independently determine the composition and list of measures necessary and sufficient to ensure the security of personal data;
privacy.operator-right-3: continue processing personal data without the user's consent where grounds provided for by the legislation of the Russian Federation exist.
privacy.operator-obligation-1: provide the user, upon their request, with information concerning the processing of their personal data;
privacy.operator-obligation-2: organize the processing of personal data in the manner established by the current legislation of the Russian Federation;
privacy.operator-obligation-3: respond to user enquiries regarding the processing of their personal data;
privacy.operator-obligation-4: take the necessary legal, organizational and technical measures to protect personal data from unlawful or accidental access, destruction, alteration, blocking, copying and dissemination;
privacy.operator-obligation-5: publish this Policy in open access on the Site.
privacy.user-title: 4. Rights and obligations of the user
privacy.user-rights-subtitle: The user has the right to:
privacy.user-right-1: receive information concerning the processing of their personal data;
privacy.user-right-2: request the correction, blocking or destruction of their personal data if it is incomplete, outdated, inaccurate or unlawfully obtained;
privacy.user-right-3: withdraw their consent to the processing of personal data at any time;
privacy.user-right-4: appeal against actions or omissions of the Operator to the authorized body for the protection of the rights of personal data subjects or in court.
privacy.user-obligation-1: provide the Operator with reliable information about themselves when signing in to the Site;
privacy.user-obligation-2: promptly inform the Operator of any changes to the personal data provided.
privacy.principles-text: The Operator processes personal data based on the following principles: lawfulness of the purposes and methods of processing; processing only data that meets specific, predetermined and lawful purposes; the inadmissibility of combining databases whose processing is carried out for incompatible purposes; the content and volume of the data processed must correspond to the stated purposes of processing; the inadmissibility of processing data that is excessive in relation to the stated purposes; ensuring the accuracy and relevance of the data; storing personal data for no longer than is required by the purposes of its processing.
privacy.data-text: When signing in to the Site via third-party services (Google, Yandex, VK), the Operator receives and stores the following profile data: e-mail address, name (display name) and, if available, the profile photo (avatar). Technical data may also be collected automatically: cookies, IP address, browser and device information, and activity on the Site, including depersonalized web analytics data (Yandex.Metrica).
privacy.purpose-text: Personal data is processed for the following purposes: authentication and identification of the user on the Site; sending informational e-mails about upcoming stargazing events and project activities; operating and improving the Site's functionality.
privacy.legal-text: The legal basis for processing is the user's consent, given when signing in to the Site. By signing in, the user confirms their consent to the processing of their personal data under the terms of this Policy, as well as consent to receive informational e-mails about the project's events.
privacy.transfer-title: 9. Transfer of data to third parties
privacy.transfer-text: The Operator does not sell personal data to third parties. Data is transferred only where necessary for the Site to function and in cases provided for by the legislation of the Russian Federation. Authentication is performed via third-party services (Google, Yandex, VK), whose data processing is governed by their own privacy policies. The Yandex.Metrica service is used to collect depersonalized Site traffic statistics; its data processing is governed by Yandex's privacy policy.
privacy.mailing-text: After signing in, the user is automatically subscribed to the newsletter about upcoming stargazing events. The user may unsubscribe at any time via the “Unsubscribe” link in any received e-mail or on the unsubscribe page. Unsubscribing does not affect the ability to use the Site.
privacy.cookies-text: The Site uses cookies to maintain the sign-in session, operate the service correctly, and collect depersonalized traffic statistics via Yandex.Metrica. The user can disable cookies in their browser settings, though this may limit the Site's functionality.
privacy.storage-text: Personal data is stored until the user withdraws their consent to its processing. The Operator takes the necessary organizational and technical measures to protect personal data from unlawful access, alteration, disclosure or destruction.
privacy.changes-text: The Operator may amend this Policy. A new version of the Policy takes effect once it is published on the Site, unless the new version provides otherwise.
privacy.contacts-text: For any questions related to the processing of personal data, you can contact the Operator (Orenburg-Media) at orenburgmedia@yandex.ru or by phone at +7 961 909 5656. Registered address: office 402, 11 Pobedy Ave., Orenburg, 460021, Russia.

observatory-work-1.text: The observatory is open to everyone. Anyone can use its capabilities to obtain images of celestial objects. Moreover, the site features a section

about.title: About the project
about.description: Learn more about the “Look at the Stars” project, our team and our homemade astronomical observatory. Join us and discover the beauty of space!
about.intro: , and this site is my attempt to tell you about my hobby, bring together all the information, and capture the milestones reached along the way of exploring space. Astronomy for me isn't just a pastime, but a whole world that changed my life. Thanks to it I've found incredible friends, learned a lot of new things, and realized just how vast and mysterious the universe is. This project is our joint contribution to popularizing and developing astronomy. We sincerely believe that through our efforts, space will become closer and more accessible to everyone.
about.telegram-channel: Subscribe to this project's author Telegram channel
about.team-description: We work in many different fields, but we're all united by one thing - a love of the stars and the night sky. Since 2016 we've been popularizing astronomy, organizing stargazing trips in the Orenburg region, and sidewalk astronomy evenings in Orenburg. Together we look at the stars, share knowledge and inspire others.
about.project-title: The “Look at the Stars” astronomy project
about.project-description: In 2016, in the Orenburg region, we launched the “Look at the Stars” project to make astronomy accessible to everyone. Since then, every season we host free lectures under the open sky, organize trips out of town with telescopes, and hold informal sidewalk astronomy evenings.
about.project-goal: The project's main goal is to show the grandeur and beauty of the universe and inspire people to explore space. We cover not only the Orenburg region but also neighboring regions, traveling to places with especially bright night skies. You can learn more about our current and past events on the
about.project-sidewalk: page, and for those who want to see the stars within the city, we've prepared a section
about.project-sidewalk-link: Where to look through a telescope in Orenburg
about.project-locations: Our events take place not only in the towns and villages of the Orenburg region, but also in neighboring regions. We organize field trips to remote locations where minimal light pollution allows you to fully enjoy the starry sky. Our team of amateur astronomers and volunteers shares their knowledge and experience so everyone can discover the beauty and mysteries of the universe.
about.project-lectures: One of the most important parts of the project is the lectures given by experienced amateur astronomers. We talk about scientific discoveries, the history of astronomy and its modern achievements. The lectures are delivered in a light format, where complex things are explained in simple, understandable language. This helps listeners broaden their horizons and inspires them to explore astronomy on their own.
about.project-sidewalk-astronomy: We pay special attention to sidewalk astronomy evenings. These open-air meetups take place in the most accessible spots in the city. At these events anyone can come up, look through a telescope, and learn more about what the night sky is hiding.
about.observatory-intro: This project is one of the most significant chapters of my life. Our homemade astronomical observatory is a unique phenomenon for the Orenburg region. It operates autonomously and captures images of deep-sky objects, making astronomy accessible to everyone. You can learn more about the observatory's operation and equipment in the section -
about.observatory-work-2: , where you'll find an archive of FITS files. This data can be used for education, asteroid searches, creating astrophotos, and other scientific or creative purposes.
about.observatory-photos-1: Soon we'll launch a free telescope control service so even more people can experience the beauty of the night sky. Be sure to check out the
about.observatory-photos-2: section, which brings together images obtained with the observatory and during our stargazing trips. These photos were processed by me and my friends and reflect our passion for space.
about.observatory-conclusion: This project is our contribution to popularizing astronomy. We sincerely hope it inspires you too to discover the universe!
about.your-help-intro: The development of the “Look at the Stars” project was made possible in large part thanks to the support of Gazprom Neft's “Home Towns” social investment program and the assistance of the “Orenburg Media” news agency team.
about.your-help-description: Our homemade observatory project has also found support among many caring participants. We have ambitious plans to develop this direction further, and your support plays a key role. We're grateful to everyone who helps with advice, consultations and recommendations. If we accidentally left someone out of our list of thanks, please let us know - it matters a lot to us.
about.your-help-conclusion: Thanks to your help, space really is becoming closer!

stargazing-tickets.important1: The number of spots on our trips is limited - we recommend registering for stargazing trips in advance.
stargazing-tickets.important2: The project is weather-dependent - events may be canceled or rescheduled due to bad weather, so watch for updates.
stargazing-tickets.important3: You'll need to make your own way to the site - unfortunately, we don't provide buses.
stargazing-tickets.important4: Take a close look at the other sections of the site if you still have questions, and be sure to subscribe to our Telegram.
stargazing-tickets.title: Tickets and project support
stargazing-tickets.description: Buy tickets for stargazing trips, seasonal passes, and support the Miksoft Astro astronomy project.
stargazing-tickets.hero-subtitle: Join our trips under the starry sky! Choose a convenient way to participate and help develop the astronomy community together with us.
stargazing-tickets.option1-title: Buy a ticket for one event
stargazing-tickets.option2-title: Buy a season pass for the whole 2026 season
stargazing-tickets.step1-text: Choose the option that works for you: a ticket for one event or a season pass.
stargazing-tickets.step2-text: For a single-event ticket, choose a convenient date from the schedule.
stargazing-tickets.step3-text: Provide your details to issue the ticket and stay in touch about the event.
stargazing-tickets.step4-text: Choose a payment method and complete the purchase on a secure page.
stargazing-tickets.step5-text: The ticket will be sent to your email. Check your “Inbox” and “Spam” folders.
stargazing-tickets.contacts-desc: We're always in touch and happy to help!

stargazing.program-arrival-desc: Arrive by car 15-20 minutes before the start and take a comfortable spot in the designated area of the field.
stargazing.program-lecture-desc: As dusk falls - an engaging lecture about space on a big screen, about an hour long.
stargazing.program-sky-desc: We learn to spot bright stars and constellations right in the night sky overhead.
stargazing.program-telescope-desc: Several telescopes are aimed at different objects - take a look and ask the astronomers your questions.
stargazing.no-photos: No photos have been uploaded from this event yet. Check back later!
stargazing.archive-confirm-body: Are you sure? The event will be hidden from users.
stargazing.title: Stargazing trips
stargazing.rules_link: Rules of conduct on stargazing trips
stargazing.rules_card_desc: What not to do and how to respect others
stargazing.howto_card_desc: The evening's program, telescopes and lectures
stargazing.where_link: Where to look through a telescope
stargazing.description: Stargazing trips in Orenburg - exciting trips out of town, where telescopes open up cosmic horizons. Evenings of scientific discoveries, lectures and lively conversation with like-minded people. Learn the rules of conduct and get ready to observe the stars.
stargazing.text: Imagine: a night sky free of city lights, thousands of stars overhead, and the rings of Saturn in a telescope's eyepiece. Our out-of-town stargazing trips aren't just observations - they're evenings that change the way you see the world. Join us to see the universe with your own eyes!

index.title: The "Look at the Stars" project
index.description: Look at the Stars is a unique project in the Orenburg region: telescope observations out of town, sidewalk astronomy, an observatory in the Orenburg region, and astrophotos

mailing.title: Newsletters
mailing.save-first-hint: Save the draft first to upload an image
mailing.launch-confirm: Are you sure? Emails will be sent to subscribers.
mailing.delete-confirm-text: This action cannot be undone. The draft will be permanently deleted.

objects.title: List of astronomical objects
objects.description: A catalog of astronomical objects captured by the observatory: galaxies, nebulae, asteroids and comets. The object table includes information on total exposure, availability of processed photos, and data for each filter: Luminance, Red, Green, Blue, Ha, OIII, SII. Learn more about each object and the observation details recorded by the observatory.

observatory-history.title: Observatory history
observatory-history.description: The history of building and developing an amateur astronomical observatory: from the very first stake in the ground to remote telescope control.

observatory.title: Observatory
observatory.description: A homemade amateur astronomical observatory with remote access from anywhere in the world via the internet. Observatory operation statistics, number of frames captured and data accumulated. Telescope operating calendar.
observatory.text: Observing the universe starts here - in real time you can see the current weather conditions, camera feeds, equipment status and imaging schedule. The observatory operates in a semi-automatic mode, allowing remote deep-sky imaging. It features an intelligent control system that analyzes weather conditions and automatically decides when to start operating. You can learn more about the equipment and how it works on a separate page:

observatory-weather.title: Weather at the Orenburg Observatory
observatory-weather.description: Current weather charts from the weather station of an amateur observatory on the outskirts of Orenburg. Temperature, humidity, cloudiness and wind speed in real time.

photo.description: {{photoTitle}} - a photo of an astronomical object taken at a homemade observatory, equipment used: {{equipment}}.

photos.title: Astrophotos
photos.description: A collection of astrophotos of galaxies, star clusters, nebulae, planets and other celestial objects captured by an amateur observatory. Find and filter images by category and parameters to see unique images of the universe with details for each filter and exposure.
